L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where a user-controlled database name is directly concatenated into a SQL query string without sanitization or use of prepared statements, allowing an attacker to manipulate the query and execute arbitrary SQL commands.

L3.area: MySQL database provisioning and lifecycle management logic
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where a user-controlled database name is directly concatenated into a SQL query used for database creation without sanitization or use of prepared statements, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL commands via a crafted database name.

ground_truth.reason: The createDatabase method directly concatenates the user-supplied database name into a SQL query string without sanitization, allowing SQL injection via a crafted database name.
